full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Implementing the DCD algorithm in Python from the paper equations
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Implement Algorithm 1 from the paper 'A Dual Coordinate Descent Method for Large-scale Linear SVM' (Hsieh et al. ICML 2008) in Python/NumPy. Include comments mapping each code block to the paper equations.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided the initial code structure. I verified and corrected the gradient computation (Eq. 10+12), the projected gradient computation (Eq. 8 for stopping check), and the L2-SVM Q-bar diagonal formula (Eq. 4).
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Corrected the projected gradient computation for the L2-SVM case (U=inf case), fixed the stopping condition to include both M-m<eps AND |M|<eps AND |m|<eps per Footnote 5 of the paper, and added convergence history tracking.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified the update rule against Eq. (9)-(11) and Eq. (12) of the paper. Verified convergence behavior by checking monotone objective decrease.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Writing the markdown annotations for each code block
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For each code block in the DCD implementation, write 2-3 sentence annotations explaining what the code computes and citing the specific equation from the paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Provided annotation templates; I edited each one to ensure the equation references are correct.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Corrected equation references (e.g., changed generic 'Eq. 4' to specific 'Eq. 9-11' for the sub-problem solve) and added specific paper section references.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Each annotation was verified against the corresponding paper section.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: What plots should I include to verify the DCD algorithm behavior? Reference what the paper shows in its figures.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested primal objective, test accuracy, and M-m gap — consistent with Figures 3-4 of the paper.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_modification: Added log-scale for M-m gap plot which makes convergence rate more visible.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified that the three plots correspond to what the paper tracks in its experimental evaluation.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Implement Algorithm 1 from 'A Dual Coordinate Descent Method for Large-scale Linear SVM' in Python/NumPy. Include comments mapping each code block to the paper equations.
top_5_prompts.why_selected: Core implementation prompt — most impactful for Task 2.2.

top_5_prompts.prompt: For each code block in the DCD implementation, write 2-3 sentence annotations explaining what the code computes and citing the specific equation from the paper.
top_5_prompts.why_selected: Task 2.2 is graded primarily on code-to-paper annotations.

top_5_prompts.prompt: What plots should I include to verify the DCD algorithm behavior?
top_5_prompts.why_selected: Led to the three-panel convergence plot design.

student_declaration.statement: I declare this is a complete record of LLM usage for Task 2.2. The DCD implementation was verified by checking monotone objective decrease and comparison with sklearn LinearSVC.
